title: cleaning charge scam
text: Be aware before renting from Avis.  My first experience with Avis turned into a very disappointing one. I rented a vehicle for $195, but was later charged an additional $125 for “cleaning” over what amounted to minor dust and dirt. Charging such a significant fee for normal post-rental condition feels excessive and unreasonable.  After being a loyal Enterprise customer for over 5 years, I have never experienced anything like this. This experience has completely changed my opinion of Avis, and I will not be renting from them again.  If you’re considering renting from Avis, inspect the vehicle carefully, take photos before and after your rental, and be aware that you could face hefty cleaning charges. Rent at your own risk.

title: Rashad and Kumani at Avis on Powder Springs are terrible
text: I am a loyal Avis customer and rent vehicles frequently. Unfortunately, my experience with this location was incredibly frustrating and highlighted what appears to be a serious breakdown in both customer service and communication.  The day before my rental, I spoke with Kumani Owens and explained my situation. He instructed me to call the branch approximately one hour before my 5:00 PM pickup time to check on vehicle availability.  Wanting to be proactive, I actually called the branch the morning of my rental as well, knowing that I would also call back an hour before pickup as Kumani had instructed. I was trying to avoid any surprises and ensure that a vehicle would be available.  Following Kumani’s instructions, I called again approximately one hour before my scheduled pickup and spoke with Rashad, the manager. When I asked whether a vehicle would be available, his response was simply, “Probably not.” He did not say my reservation was canceled, nor did he definitively tell me that a vehicle would not be available.  Concerned by the uncertainty, I contacted Avis Customer Service. I spoke with two separate representatives who advised me that the branch should be attempting to locate a vehicle if inventory was limited rather than simply leaving a customer with a confirmed reservation without options.  Shortly afterward, I received the following text message directly from Avis:  “Your AVIS reservation number 1802*****has been confirmed.”  Like any reasonable customer, I interpreted that message to mean exactly what it said: my reservation had been confirmed.  Based on the information I had received, I went to the branch expecting that Avis would honor the reservation that had been confirmed through its own system. Instead, I was told there was no vehicle available.  What happened next was even more disappointing. Kumani acted as though I should have somehow known that the confirmation message was not valid because he believed Rashad had already told me there would not be a vehicle. That is simply not what happened. Rashad never gave me a definitive answer. He said “probably not,” and after that conversation I received an official Avis confirmation text stating that my reservation had been confirmed.  Rather than acknowledging the conflicting information I had been given or understanding why I reasonably believed a vehicle would be available, both employees were dismissive and unwilling to take ownership of the situation. There was no apology, no attempt to help locate a vehicle, and no effort to provide a meaningful solution.  A confirmed reservation should mean something. Customers should not have to guess whether a confirmation message from Avis is real, nor should they be expected to know that a branch has decided not to honor a reservation. At a minimum, customers deserve clear communication, professional treatment, and accountability when things go wrong.  This review is based not only on this incident but on multiple interactions with this branch. The recurring themes have been poor communication, lack of customer service, and an apparent indifference toward customer concerns. Avis deserves better representation than what I have consistently experienced at this location.  I hope the owner will take action and hire someone that can atleast get their poor customer service rating above a 4.0.

title: Worst reservation experience
text: I had an extremely disappointing and frustrating experience with Avis. I booked a car at one location expecting the reservation to be honored, but instead I was sent to three different locations. I had to pay for cabs out of my own pocket just to keep chasing a car that Avis had already confirmed but clearly did not have available.  What made the experience worse was the complete lack of coordination and responsibility. Avis did not properly verify inventory before confirming or redirecting me. Each location I was sent to was sold out, which wasted my time, money, and energy. This is completely unacceptable from a company that is supposed to provide reliable transportation.  A reservation should mean that a car is actually available, not that the customer is forced to run around the city from one location to another with no solution. The staff and system showed poor communication, poor inventory management, and no respect for the customer’s time or expense.  This was one of the worst rental car experiences I have ever had. Avis failed at the most basic part of the service: providing the car I booked. I would not recommend Avis to anyone unless they are prepared to waste time, spend extra money on transportation, and still end up without a vehicle.

title: Horrible customer service and policy
text: I was planning a beach vacation with my family and booked a rental car through the app under my name. Unfortunately, due to recent events and my military obligations, I’m no longer able to go on the trip with them. I contacted customer service to see if the reservation could simply be changed to my wife’s name. We share the same last name and the reservation is tied to the same credit card, so I assumed this would be a straightforward request. Instead, I was told it wasn’t possible. The first representative I spoke with was extremely unpleasant and completely unwilling to help. When I asked to speak with a supervisor, I was told, “I can, but they’re going to tell you the same thing.” When I finally did speak with a supervisor, that turned out to be exactly the case. The only option they offered was to cancel the reservation and rebook it under my wife’s name—which, because the trip is only two days away, would now cost an additional $500. There was no effort to accommodate the situation, no attempt to work with a loyal customer, and frankly no concern at all. What makes this especially frustrating is that I have been a very loyal Avis customer. Over the past six months alone, I’ve rented with them for more than 90 days. Despite that loyalty—and despite the fact that this request was simply to change the name to my spouse using the same payment method—I was met with rigid policies and some of the worst customer service I’ve experienced. Because of this experience, I will not be using Avis again. Between the inflexible policy and the absolutely terrible customer service from both the initial representative and the supervisor, this was incredibly disappointing. As someone who travels and rents cars frequently, I expected far better.

When travel disruptions are caused by severe weather and government-declared emergencies, customers shouldn’t be penalized. This wasn’t a last-minute cancellation for convenience — it was a forced situation due to unsafe travel conditions.  It’s frustrating that a company of this size can’t show basic consideration during an emergency. Situations like this make customers reconsider who they book with in the future.  Very disappointing experience.

title: Bad business
text: So I recently had to use budget car rental because my usual rental place was out of one way rentals. No problem, right? I got the car, I drove to my destination, dropped off the car with a full tank of gas. Alls good, until I get a bill in the mail, a couple weeks later, for a damaged windshield. $800.00!! And I only got ONE notice in the mail. It’s already gone to collections. It’s April 14. I returned the car on Feb 26th. The thing is, my normal rental place comes outside with me to inspect every time I return a car. Budget has not done that once! I’ve only rented two times with them, and then this bill. Maybe try inspecting the cars when they are returned, not whenever yall get around to it. Who knows who walked by this car and messed up the windshield? And on that note, I checked my records, and I had the “cover the car” add on insurance too. So I’m at a loss here. But I know I’ll be disputing those charges for sure. And, I’ll avoid using Budget again.

title: Horrible company
text: I had an absolutely terrible experience with Budget at MCO Airport, and I would strongly advise others to avoid this location.  I was charged $150 for a rental car that I never even picked up. My flight with United Airlines was delayed, and during that same timeframe Budget was experiencing internet/system maintenance issues. Because of this, I was unable to cancel or modify my reservation. Despite these circumstances being completely out of my control, I was still charged—completely unreasonable.  To make matters worse, the staff at the MCO counter were rude, dismissive, and unwilling to help. There was no attempt to understand the situation or provide any level of customer service. I was treated like an inconvenience rather than a paying customer.  This entire experience was frustrating, unprofessional, and honestly felt predatory. Charging customers under these conditions while providing such poor service is unacceptable.  I will never use Budget again, and I will be actively discouraging my family and friends from doing so as well. There are far better rental companies that actually value their customers.  Avoid this location if you care about fair treatment and basic professionalism.

title: Unable to use the app when I need to
text: I rarely write app reviews but this was such an egregious offense that I felt I must leave my feedback for the developers.  I downloaded this Budget app before my international flight. I logged in before leaving and all of my rental information was there, ready for me when I arrived at my destination.   After landing in the foreign country and collecting my bags, I opened the Budget app as I walked to the rental counter. Instead of displaying my rental details, the Budget app presented me with a blocking modal screen that informed me that an app update was available. However, there was no way to dismiss this blocking message. The only option available was to download the updated app from the App Store, which I could not do doing over my cellular connection on international roaming.   It is absolutely absurd to block the use of the app until an update can be downloaded. I wasn’t even using a very old version of it anyway, I had downloaded this one 2 days prior.   Developers: provide a way to allow use of the Budget app even if there is an update available. As it stands now the app is completely useless when this situation arises.

title: I’m refundable early vehicle return
text: On February 19, I rented a vehicle through Budget with a scheduled return date of March 1. The total charge was $493.00 and some cents. However, after months of vehicle issues, my personal car was unexpectedly repaired sooner than anticipated, and I returned the rental on February 21—after approximately 2½ days, not even three full days.  At the time of return, I was explicitly told that the difference would be refunded. I returned the vehicle in proper condition and well within policy expectations. Despite this, I am now being informed that no refund will be issued and that Budget intends to keep the entire amount.  This is deeply disappointing and feels dishonest, especially given the assurances made at the time of return and my history as a frequent and responsible renter. Retaining the full amount for a rental that was returned early is not reasonable, nor does it reflect the level of service I would expect from you all.  I am deeply deeply dissatisfied!

title: Pay later feature
text: I submitted an application to use Budget’s “Pay Later” option. I received an offer with terms, accepted it, and then the app suddenly logged me out. When I signed back in, the offer had disappeared. I called customer service, provided my account number and all my information, but they couldn’t find any record of the application or offer. I then went to a Budget location in person. The representative seemed completely clueless about what I was talking about, yet he immediately asked for my credit card despite my obvious confusion. I hope I never have to deal with Budget again.

title: Change Reservation
text: I wanted to change my rental by extending it by one day. I went on the app which I use to book frequently.   There is no place under your current rental where it says or allows you to change the reservation.   After going through the automated route of trying to extend, that didn’t work. (Mind you I’m only trying to extend by one day. Drop off the next day at the same time and same location). I then had to go through ALL of the information AGAIN with a live agent that made things even more frustrating. Regardless, my total rental (6 days) was $380. For adding exactly 24 more hours, it was over $100.  And although not an enormous amount of money, that is a ~35% increase compared to the entire rental. Ridiculous. - rethinking Budget in the future now. This is for being a loyal customer.

title: Dangerous car!
text: We rented a car at London Heathrow on 17 August ‘25 and they sent us on our way. We drove to some very remote places in both England and Scotland, while in Edinburgh at 9pm while preparing to leave for Glasgow the car wouldn’t start and budget was no help as all they suggested was for me to tow it to the airport (office closed) to get another car. I called AA who asked if this was the same battery problem that was reported on the 17th as the names didn’t match we surmised he reported the problem and nothing was done and they rented an unsafe car to me.  AA came and all they could do was give me a jump start which I did then drove to Glasgow without shutting it off. The budget office luckily was a 10 min walk from my hotel but they wouldn’t give me another car even though there were people renting them, but told me to tow it to the airport. No pickup and swap, no customer service, no help! AA came out again and I had to get him to install a new battery. A very disappointing experience and I lost a whole day of my vacation! Plus if this had happened when my family and I were in the highlands of Scotland it could have had a totally different out come.

title: Baseless charges
text: Several days after returning a rental car, I received an email informing me that I would be charged $450 for a cleaning fee. The email listed potential reasons a cleaning fee could be applied, but said nothing about the actual condition of the vehicle I returned. I responded to the email for clarification, was told there was no charge showing on their end, and it must have been a mistake.   A few days later, there was a charge on my account, and I received a letter in the mail (it was identical to the email in its lack of explanation for the charge).   I called Budget to get more information, and was told that it was because the vehicle smelled like smoke and someone must have been smoking in the car. I do not smoke. Neither does my wife, nor my three young children. I told the agent that. She turned on a dime, saying that “oh, actually, it’s because of dust and dirt particles then”. It seemed clear to me that Budget just makes random charges hoping that people won’t bother disputing them. I told the agent I needed the charge refunded. She told me that she could give me a small discount “because the charge is valid”.   I called my credit card company to dispute the charge immediately.   Randomly accusing your customers of damaging your vehicles is bad enough, but not correcting the charge when it was questioned (and when the charge was, at best, made in error) is poor customer service and a bad business practice.

title: Potentially racially motivated account deletion
text: I don’t usually write reviews but I made an account because I had an important meeting to catch that was far from me and an uber would’ve been too expensive. My colleagues recommended Zipcar to me so I downloaded it, set it up scanned my DL and took the selfie. Day before my meeting my account gets “marked for review” for no reason I haven’t had the account for a full day yet AND then the day of it was “temporarily suspended.” I carried customer service and the last I talked with explained it was a “security procedure” to flag and suspend user accounts for no reason with no warning making them unable to use their app and that just makes no sense to me because none of these reviews mention it. Then this morning I get an email saying my account was deleted (again mind you, I never got a chance to add my payment information, let alone book a car). Needless to say I’m deleting the app and telling everyone I know to use hertz or uber rentals instead. I called ZipCar to find out what was going on and was told my account was suspended pending an investigation and to call back on Monday, April 6,2026. I called back and was told that the account was closed and I was banned from using ZipCar permanently. I asked why and was never given an explanation. I asked to speak to a supervisor and was told there wasn’t any supervisor. I was told if I wanted to appeal the decision I could respond to an email that was to be sent out. I got the email and responded to it only to get an error message saying that the email could not be responded to. I attempted to contact Zipcar through their Facebook account and got the run around. title: On of the Greatest Smart Mobility Solutions
text: Zipcar has consistently been one of the most practical and innovative mobility solutions available. The ability to reserve a vehicle by the hour or day through a simple app makes transportation flexible, affordable, and convenient without the costs and responsibilities of car ownership.  The vehicles are generally clean, well-maintained, and located in convenient areas throughout the city. For professionals, students, travelers, and occasional drivers, Zipcar provides an excellent balance of accessibility, reliability, and value. The reservation process is straightforward, pickup and return are seamless, and the variety of vehicle options helps meet different transportation needs.  As urban transportation continues to evolve, Zipcar remains a leading example of smart, sustainable, and user-friendly mobility. Highly recommended for anyone seeking convenient on-demand transportation.

They’ve recently shifted from providing you with a physical key to having a key fob locked and hidden somewhere within the vehicle. When attempting to start the car again on our journey the key fob battery was dead and the car would no longer start. I figured that’s a $5 fix for a new battery.   I contacted customer support and was told they could not disclose where the key is located in the car for “security purposes” and would have to arrange for the vehicle to be towed. However rather than being able to use the tow company a mile down the road, they could only use their authorized tow company which would take a few days to pick up the vehicle. They essentially left my wife and I stranded. What could had been a $5 battery replacement turned into multiple hundred of dollars to tow the vehicle. I do not understand why Zipcar will gladly abandon their vehicles and their customers for days on end.

title: Do not use Zipcar, use Turo

When I returned that car, there was a car parked in the Zipcar spot! I parked nearby, called Zipcar, told them where I parked it (a short term spot), and then a month later got emailed a parking ticket for that spot! title: They really don’t value customer loyalty
text: I’ve been a customer of this service for a few years now. At first, things were okay, but over time, I ran into a lot of issues. They took unauthorized funds from my account more than once due to mileage miscalculations and took days to fix it without offering any meaningful compensation.  When I moved to Dallas, it got even more frustrating. I’d arrive at a location only to find out the car wasn’t available. The whole point of this service is supposed to be renting a car on demand, and that’s the last thing I’ve experienced. Instead of getting a quick, convenient rental, I’ve had to deal with constant miscommunications and last-minute changes.  On top of that, there’s a real lack of membership loyalty. They’re quick to collect monthly fees, but when it comes to offering real solutions or compensation, they fall short. Most of the time, I had to be the one to suggest canceling because they wouldn’t offer any real resolution.  In short, this service could be so much better if they actually focused on the core promise of on-demand rentals and valued their loyal customers. Right now, it just doesn’t feel like they do.

title: Unreliable
text: I’ve been using Zipcar on and off for about two years, and unfortunately my experiences have been unreliable. On multiple occasions, Zipcar has taken money that didn’t belong to them and, while they did eventually refund it, the process always came with poor customer service.  The first issue happened when I booked a 7-hour trip (11 p.m. to 7 a.m.), but their system changed my reservation from a 6-hour booking down to only 1 hour. I was still charged the 6-hour price for that shortened trip, and then they tried to charge me again for the original 7-hour reservation. They actually took the money from my account before eventually refunding it.  The second issue came when they incorrectly charged me $128, claiming I didn’t return the car to the right location. In reality, I returned it on time and to the correct spot. They did refund the money, but not without putting a hold on my account. After the hold was lifted, they then deleted my account, saying I hadn’t paid the membership fee — even though I couldn’t pay it because of the hold.  Overall, while Zipcar did eventually return my money, the repeated billing errors, account issues, and poor customer service make the service unreliable.

title: Poor Communication and Unfair Treatment
text: I want to leave a clear record of my experience with Zipcar. After canceling my membership years ago (and even receiving a refund at the time), Zipcar continued to charge me annual fees without providing any service. When I contacted support, the communication was slow, inconsistent, and dismissive.  At first, Zipcar admitted the mistake and refunded one charge, but then refused to refund earlier charges even though I provided proof of my cancellation and refund history. The company’s explanations contradicted themselves — they refunded my membership in one year while claiming my account was still “active” in another. This contradiction was never explained, and my concerns were brushed off as “policy.”  In the end, they marked my case as “resolved” without ever fairly addressing the real issue. This kind of communication and refusal to take responsibility is unacceptable for a company holding people’s payment information.  If you’re thinking about using Zipcar, be very careful — keep written records, check your statements, and do not assume “cancellation” means what it should. My experience shows how poor communication and a lack of accountability can lead to years of unfair charges.

title: Dirty Cars, Dirty Tactics
text: I am a college student and having ZipCar(s) near my campus seemed like a great thing for me to use, pretty affordable and easy access in the case that I needed to drive anywhere. Unfortunately I have come to figure out that the cars are 1. Not well maintained, and 2. Not clean. I rented a simple Kia Forte for an hour and a half to run an errand, I was met with a car with visual damage that seemed a little off putting. The damage itself wasn’t enough for me to reject the car, as it was mostly cosmetic (cracks in front and rear bumpers, tennis ball sized dents on multiple locations) and so I continued with the inspection process. When I opened the car though, the smell was horrible. I thought ZipCars were supposed to be non smoking vehicles? I was met with the distinct and very recognizable smell of a car that has had a smoke smell MARINATE. It’s not a recent event in that car’s life. The car itself was also dirty on the inside, crumbs lining the floor, coins scattered in the central storage, etc. I made SURE to note all these defects in my pre-trip inspection. Unfortunately for me I couldn’t cancel and rebook as this was the only car available at the time and my errand was urgent and couldn’t wait. I had to drive with the windows down in 60 degree weather to make the smell manageable. At the end of my rental I attempt to contact the text support but am frankly left unsatisfied, so I revisit it in the morning. Support not only proceeds to have extensive waiting times for responses but also just ghosts me for around 6 hours until I’m fed up enough to call in person. While seething in the fact that support left me unanswered for so long I check my bank account, now tell me why, as a broke college student I see not one, not two, but THREE separate charges for the same amount on my card? (Thankfully one of them seemed to have been removed) Now I sure as heck do not have movement like that and can overlook an extra 30-40 bucks missing from my account. So this is when I call support. The lady in which I speak with is dejected and frankly unhelpful. When I mentioned my previous chats with text support I audibly heard her SIGH as if I’m wasting her time and day trying to solve the issue?  I was nothing but kind and respectful as I know tech support is an exhausting job to deal with but that doesn’t mean I’m going to take it kindly when you seem unhappy to DO YOUR JOB. Hopefully this app can fix itself and its ways, my money better be returned in my account in the “3-5 business days” as stated otherwise there will be a big issue, as well as the “30 minute credit” they promised as a result of the frankly nasty car. I hope others have a more positive and pleasant experience with this rental app than I have had but I think I’ll stick to walking, public transport, or other means of travel.

text: I have rented from Zipcar three times now, and each of those three times it has been BLATANTLY OBVIOUS that smoking—and I mean a lot of smoking—has taken place in the car. In the last rental, it is CLEAR  that whoever was smoking in the passenger seat dropped there weed because there are clearly marijuana bits all on the floor. I was tempted to stop and clean the entire car myself, but it’s obvious that the next person would just dirty it up again.   Also, the cars are not well maintained at all—does anyone clean them? I had to stop at Walmart to by wipes for the windows. The windows were so dirty that, at night, I was literally afraid I was going to die because I couldn’t see anything!!!!!   And let’s not begin to talk about the sound of the engine in that Camry I got.   That said, if you NEED a quick car, this service will help you but it will NOT be a comfortable ride unless you smoke weed everyday and you like dirt.

title: SCAM
text: I rented a car last week and went to park in the public parking spot in my neighborhood, only to find it was blocked by another Zipcar and I had nowhere to park. I spent over an hour driving around looking for parking spots (on the phone with Zipcar support the whole time) where they recommended I park in a lot 1-4 miles away and 'walk home' despite renting the car amidst a busy day and nursing an injury. They promised repeatedly they were filing this incident on my account and I would not be charged if the car was towed or received parking tickets, only to be charged $100+ to both extend the car (which the employee lied and said was free since I spent the hour looking for parking and at a huge inconvenience to em) as well as for the parking ticket. I'm grateful I knew better than to leave it parked in a tow away zone because these scammers would've charged me $1k+ for that issue. After 5 calls, 3 emails they are now saying they don't have any record of the issue on my file (despite multiple employees seeing it before) and demanding I share evidence (which I don't have because it's a phone call). I just cancelled my membership and reported the fraud to my bank and the better business bureau. Do not use this company - they will charge you exorbitantly for things ttheir employees tell you to do, while refusing to provide any credit for the inconvenience and time lost (both trying to park and 5x calls later).

title: do not use zip car
text: if you're thinking about using zip car you should definitely reconsider you would save money by using Uber or Lyft and it would be much more convenient. title: The app is not working correctly.
text: It's too expensive. Customer support isn't very good. If a user has problems with the car or the app, you'll have to wait a long time for help from customer support. The last time I called the support call center, I waited for 40 minutes. I couldn’t start the car because the app was glitching. Meanwhile, the rental time had already started, and I could only wait for a response from support. After 40 minutes, they answered the call and gave me another car from the same parking lot, adjusting the rental time to account for the delay. However, they didn’t refund me for the 40 minutes I had already spent waiting for support, and I was still charged for that time. Essentially, they just moved the rental period later, as if I had started the rental later, but I still paid for those 40 minutes of waiting. Later, when I tried to return the car on time, the app glitched again and wouldn’t let me end the rental, even though I arrived at the return location on time. The app kept tracking time and charged me for the extra minutes while I was trying to contact support again. Despite returning the car on time, they never refunded me for the extra charges for the time I couldn’t end the rental due to the app issues. It seems like this company is irresponsible toward its customers. They should follow basic service standards, like those in European car-sharing services. Despite the highest rental prices, this service is the most frustrating. Every time I used it, something went wrong.

title: ABSOLUTE NIGHTMARE - STAY AWAY FROM THIS APP!
text: Where do I even begin with this disaster? First, their registration system is completely broken and poorly designed. Made ONE simple mistake with my phone number during registration? Are we living in the stone age? The customer service is a whole other level of incompetence. They kept sending copy-paste responses for TWO WEEKS, completely ignoring the actual problem. And here's the kicker - when I finally gave up and asked to delete my account, they had the audacity to tell me it would take SIX MONTHS! Yes, you read that right. SIX. MONTHS. The UX is so horrifically bad that I couldn't even get past the login screen. Their form doesn't properly handle country codes, leading to verification failures, and there's absolutely no way to fix it without jumping through ridiculous hoops. Save yourself the headache and use literally ANY other car-sharing service. I've never seen such a backwards, user-hostile system in my life. Deleting this app and never looking back. Absolutely pathetic service in 2025."

title: Only use as a last resort; even then check ALL other options first.
text: The below is from a recent trip end report which is just one among many annoyances using this service. I’ve been using this service on and off for almost two years and it’s always been a pain.  Often cars are dirty and smelly also. And more than once l've had to refill nearly flat tires. In between personal cars I have to use it for longer errands but as soon as I have another option best believe this will be cancelled. Also you have to pay the $25 application fee anytime you cancel and reactivate and no options for pausing. Total ripoff and scam!!  When checking out the car, I had to do the full inspection 4-5 times because the app would not let me submit. Finally I started going through and choosing the boxes that would let me submit. Only certain combinations of checked box and text in box would let me submit. In the end in fact I don’t think I was even able to leave a text comment. Overall, if I didn’t need this service (and I will unsubscribe as soon as I don’t) I wouldn’t be using it at all. The experience itself plus customer service has been awful. I recently had to carry $1000 of damages on a work trip because the support team would not answer my questions or help out at all even though it was a hit and run at the location the zipcar was always parked. Absolute nightmare. If anything I’ll actively tell people NOT to use zipcar unless their life depended on it.

You have to fill up on gas on your own time. You have to fill up tires on your own time. End up being late for any of the issues above you get fined 50$ even though it’s a subscription service and youre paying the insurance and the cars are all booked when you need them.   Customer service can help solve your problems when you get to them but they never give you credits or refunds.   The system itself works and has helped me a lot but ive had several really bad experiences that i received no compensation or consideration for that has prompted me to leave this review.

title: Crapshoot Capitalism
text: If you book, know that your car may not be there. When you get there, only to find it’s not, the response of this corporation is to refund what you paid and give you a credit for an additional hour, to be used within the next 60 days. Why allow a customer to book a car the company knows is not available?   Having used the service a lot, it’s clear this is a business practice. title: DO NOT USE FOR UBER!!!!!!
text: They will charge you $154 for being EXACTLY ontime and even 1 minute late.. they say call and tell them if you’re running behind so they can not charge you but THEY STILL CHARGE YOU! Here is my situation, I booked the car for a couple of days to do Uber. As time is getting closer to drop back off the car I attempted to extend it, it lets me. (This is where it gets very very frustrating and honestly a scam). I then get a notification at 2:14pm while doing a Uber ride (mind you the car WAS due at 2:30pm). So I call and get everything clarified, was told that I won’t be charged and if so call them and get it fixed. So when I dropped the car off and got into another I waited 2 hours just to see who booked my car when there was 2 other cars available. Waited, and waited, finally I check the app to see if the car was really booked… lol no one booked the car, I booked it again and they cancelled my reservation the same day. Asked why and get told a different answer everytime. Now I call to ask for the $154 to be removed and guess what I’m told? Sorry we can’t do that. I pointed out the notes that were left and still get told no we can’t do that. Then I mentioned what occurred and that the car is still there unbooked even tho it showed I was booked in it, then someone else overrided my bookin, SOMEHOW-still don’t know how that was possible. NOW, the car is not booked and free?!?! You see the scam in all of this, almost like they wanted not only the $99 I paid to rebook but charged me an extra $154 to get even more money from me. If it’s not removed by 9pm Oct. 29 I am taking Zipcar and whatever corporation that runs them to court. I’m sure I’m not the first this has happened to but I will promise you, I will be the last.  Update: 3 agents hung up on me in my face after saying they’ll speak to a manger. I am respectful, I did not raise my voice, I am just looking for a solution that I know can be done.. but I tried playing it nice, you guys still owe ME money so I’m just going to test my luck in court. -do not partner with this company. I as well as others will be making complaints to Uber as well as fulfilling a lawsuit.

Most likely the account closure for “lateness” would be automatic for a tech oriented company like this, so your only option is to return the car early, with an open ended buffer based on whatever return process is implemented in the app, which could fluctuate unexpectedly in the amount of time it takes. The return time should be based on when I tap the “end trip” button, with the lengthy return process not counting toward that, unless I stop interacting with the app and it times out or something, which should then require me to tap the “end trip” button again. title: Unreliable cars and jaded customer support
text: I’ve been a Zipcar member for over 15 years, and lately there has been a massive uptick in the amount of members failing to return cars on time.  I’m not talking about 5-10min late, I’m talking about HOURS.   In the 15 years I’ve been a member, I had never once experienced this once, and then suddenly in the last few months it’s happened 3-4x with the SAME location.  Zipcar used to drop members who did this, but clearly that’s not happening.  They need to increase the late fee to $500 and drop members who do it more than 2x in a 12mo period–that would probably eliminate the problem altogether.    Worse, when you call Zipcar customer support, they are super blasé about it.   I feel like that $50 late fee should be given to the member it impacted.  But customer support doesn’t seem to care.  They offer to try to find you another car nearby, often one of lesser quality and multiple blocks away—which is a problem for me as a disabled person.  Then, they proceed to charge you the SAME fee as if you got your original rental—not discounting it at all.  Even better, you actually have to ASK them to change the reservation to remove the period you paid for that the car wasn’t available for.  Today was the final straw and I cancelled my membership.  Zipcar used to be great, but it’s terrible now.

Charging a $50 late fee under these circumstances is absolutely ridiculous and shows a lack of appreciation for customers who go the extra mile. Zipcar, this is not how you retain loyal customers. I expected better understanding and support, but instead, I am left feeling frustrated and undervalued.

title: Nightmarish Zipcar Experience Portland
text: Buyer beware!  I have been using zip car since 2021 in Portland, OR.My rental experience has progressively gotten worse over time until now, in May of 2024, when I am seriously considering unsubscribing from them. I tend to rent a car from them at least twice a month.  The following factors are present in about 75% of the cars I rent from them:  1. Cleanliness: Pet hair on seats, floor, or trunk Cigarette burns on seats Stains on seats Sand on seats Sand in trunk Trash on seats Stains on center console  Trash left in trunk Trash left under the seats Trash left in door storage areas  Typically several of these cleanliness factors are at play. I rarely encounter a zip car that is relatively clean.  There is zero accountability for the filth and dirtiness that these customers tend to leave behind.  2. Issues with app: App routinely fails to properly communicate with car locks so you are unable to end the trip because the app will not lock the doors. App must be restarted because bluetooth lock command fails to communicate with the car  3. Issues with customer support Routinely deal with 20-40 minute wait times when issues with the car occur. Time of day doesn’t matter nor does priority customer route make a difference. If you have an issue with your current car and need another one, customer support reps tend not to compensate you for lost time with more time on your active trip; rather, they give you a credit for the next trip.  If you shorten your trip by accident and then end it, they will not refund you some of the trip.    4. Issues with parking spots Zip car parking spots are poorly marked with small signage. People often trespass by parking their personal vehicles in your reserved zip car spots, forcing you to park elsewhere and notify zip car of your new parking location. You are not compensated for this time wasted.  5. Issues with the cars: Cars will have issues with tires, external damage, warning lights on dashboard, etc (caused by previous customers) which I have reported in the past repeatedly with the same cars. Zipcar does not repair them nor take them out of service after my reports. They continued to rent them out despite having some of these issues. They appear to care more about the bottom line than the safety of their customers  In conclusion, the zipcars in Portland tend to be filthy and disgusting and will typically have some external or internal damage.  If that isn’t an issue, the app typically will malfunction by failing to communicate with the locks on the car.  Additionally, customer service will invariably try to cheat you out of your money by giving you a credit on future trips.  This service in it’s current state is nearly unusable.  I suggest you stay away from it.

text: I live in the city and gave up my car, figuring the few times I needed a car I could use zip car. That was my first mistake. Every single car I got smelled of disgusting cigarettes. Most of the cars were trashed with garbage everywhere, windows smeared and crumbs everywhere. Several of the cars had broken headlights, flat tires and smashed bumpers. Customer service takes forever to get ahold of and does little to help when you get in touch with them.   Bottom line, I subscribed to this service for less hassle, only it’s way more because of the condition of the cars.

title: Major loss of functionality
text: The app recently downgraded it’s functionality when it changed the interface for taking photos of the vehicle at the start and end of the rental period. Formerly, it was possible to select your camera’s wide angle lens to properly capture the car. Now it is not possible to do so. The prior version also provided you with the sections of the car’s exterior to document. No longer there. Most notably, the new version consistently fails to let you proceed past the exterior photos. Did anyone actually test this before release?

title: App not working
text: My app isn’t letting me Log In. I have an active account and paying for service. I cannot access at all from the app. I had tried deleting app, changing email, password and everything else. Nothing let me log in on the app. I can use browser but can’t open a car from the phone.  I hace iPhone 15 & last apple version.  Completely useless now my account. Thanks a lot. 😤

title: Unable to reopen account
text: Unfortunate experience, after being a loyal customer with Zipcar and relying on its services to transport my children to school & my sick grandma to and from appointments, there are times were I brought reservations a little bit past the drop off time. However, being a customer that has spent over $6,000 using the service, I would’ve hoped to receive better customer service when attempting to reopen my closed account. The team is not offering any type of way for me to reopen my account, they blankly tell me it’s permanent and I should consider other car sharing options. Highly disappointing, and I wish there was a way for members to have their account closure reconsidered in a more generous way. I wish Zipcar could give my family another chance at having reliable transportation.

title: Stuck in the middle of the street
text: So I rented a Kia Soul and everything was going good until late in the evening when I stopped at a red light and when I tried to drive it wouldn’t move.  So here I am stuck in the middle of the road and blocking traffic, so frustrating! I put it in park and then back into drive and the darn thing started rolling backwards smh I had to use the emergency break cause the break petal wouldn’t budge, thank goodness I didn’t hit the person behind me. Luckily a nice guy was driving by helped push me into the gas station lot that was right there. This experience has made me nervous about renting another vehicle from this app.

title: Finicky Bluetooth unlock
text: I’ve been stranded twice now because the app doesn’t work. Bring back the unlock cards!!

title: great concept - terrible service
text: Had panned a trip with my family to go for a birthday party. Have a new born so booked a SUV for safety reasons. Zipcar cancelled the trip the day before. The reinstated it. Then changed the car. The changed the location of the pick up. And all only over email that i had to find in my spam. After spending an hour to get a SUV, they moved the location. James Donovan from supprt said they would cover the Uber to pick up the car and get home given the change in location which i did. After claiming the Uber trip because of Zipcar changes - they denied the refund even after James has clearly said in an email that it would be refunded. Ridiculous and terrible experience. Never recommend this to anyone who wants some peace of mind and a fair treatment.

title: Non-refundability of membership/activation is a scam
text: As many other customers I saw their prices as quite reasonable before joining zipcar only get 30+% higher prices at the checkout and thus having wasted the 35$ for the membership fee and activation…

title: Buggin’
text: Guys, your app got more Bugs than Looney Tunes…

title: Customer Service intentionally doesn’t help after 40 minutes of keeping me on the line
text: It seems odd but the customer service attitude towards the customer is plain dismissive and never supporting.  I got a late fee of $50 when my phone died and took me hours to recharge and although I had dropped the car I couldn’t end the trip. After spending 40 minutes with a customer service agent who was keep vanishing on me I was told nothing can be done. This happened on two other occasions just this week. I think the app basically doesn’t allow the support agents to do anything that involves refunds. And the app does have issues, fails to extend, etc. And the $12, $16 and $50 fees add up…

title: First Ride App Doesn’t Work
text: I downloaded the app, signed up and reserved my first car. I go to pick up the car and I can’t sign onto the app. I tried resetting my password three times, uninstalling and reinstalling the app and neither would work. I called customer service and they weren’t able to help me. They refunded my trip, which is great and all, but I still needed a car for the day.  I cancelled my account without ever using it because it’s not reliable.

title: I was unknowingly charged 50$ multiple
text: They charged my account multiple times with 50 dollar late fee for being 5 minutes late for my return location. I am very regular customer, When I chatted with customer support, the agent was very unsupportive. I’ve cancelled my zip car membership. Terrible experience

title: Cars often need repairs, maintenance, and smell like smoke
text: We have tried Zipcar a three times now and have yet to have a good experience.   The first time our car was in a gated lot and we were not able to get out of the lot. After an hour of wasted time and talking to customer support they realized the building’s management had changed the gating system and they forgot to update their zipcars.  The next time we took a Zipcar a bit farther out to go for a hike and realized the car had no key, and we could only get into the car using our cell phone. We were concerned at the trailhead we might not have service and called support, and they confirmed we couldn’t get in without cell service and suggested we park somewhere else… what a useless suggestion. I’m not comfortable taking a car that doesn’t come with a key, if your phone dies or you don’t have service you are screwed.  Most recently we took a car again and upon picking it up it reeked of cigarette smoke. We didn’t want to cancel our plans so reported it in the pre trip inspection and went anyways. 5 minutes into the trip we got a warning that the rear tire was at 15 PSI, so we had to go well out of our way (not trivial in NYC) to get air for the tire. Zipcar wouldn’t refund us for any time because we didn’t call them during the issue and request a new car. Given that’s their policy I think we are done with Zipcar, it’s not reliable enough to really plan for anything because if there is an issue they want you to call them and wait around for them to try and find you some other car you need to walk blocks over to. We just paid 150 bucks to take a stinky car for 6 hours and spend 30 minutes of that time finding a gas station in NYC to pump up the tire, or cancel our plans for the day. We could just Uber for less money and fewer problems…

title: Terrible experience

title: 50% reliable

I end up needing to call for over half of the trips I take with Zipcar, and they’re always “experiencing elevated call volume.”  This company doesn’t care about self service or great customer support, and I guess it must not be good economics.  Anyways, give yourself an extra hour or two to deal with Zipcar.  Definitely 2 stars.
